message:
Revert closure fix that caused method name shadowing

The previous fix (a1054b32) tried to emit 'const foo = __m1_c_foo' for
shadowed/redefined closures, but this causes conflicts when class methods
have the same name as outer closures.

Example failure (test_abc):
  def foo(self): pass  # closure in method scope
  const foo = __m1_c_foo;  # emitted by fix
  class C:
    def foo(self): return 3  # ERROR: shadows outer 'foo'

Root cause: Zig struct methods can't shadow outer scope constants.
Python allows this because method namespace != variable namespace.

Solution: DON'T emit original name assignment for shadowed/redefined closures.
The unique alias (__m1_c_foo) is sufficient - calls work via var_renames.

Result:
- test_abc: compile error -> runtime (fixed!)
- test_call: still compiles and runs (no regression)
- Compile failures: 38 -> 36 (2 tests unblocked)
